q: Contrast Confucius and Laozi on how one should live and govern. Cite a concrete claim from each corpus.
final:
Confucius advocated for governing by virtue and example, emphasizing self-cultivation. A concrete claim from his corpus is: "Confucius — govern by virtue and example, having first rectified yourself; the rectification of names instead of coercion."

Laozi, in contrast, promoted a style of governance characterized by minimal intervention, as stated: "Laozi — the best ruler is one the people barely know exists: the more prohibitions and meddling, the people become poorer." He believed in allowing things to follow their natural course, as exemplified by the claim: "Laozi — things go of themselves, and the movement of the Tao is return: do not pull at the shoots; the plant will grow."
